events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need identify component/condition: harness impact indicator deployed (webbing tag). In fall protection, harness impact indicator is a load-indicating device/tag that deploys/rips when harness has been subjected to fall arrest forces. Once deployed, harness must be removed from service per OSHA 1910.140(c)(14) and manufacturer instructions. It is a defective/failed condition. Work stage rough-complete. Decision fail. Risk high/critical? A deployed impact indicator indicates harness involved in fall or improper loading; if used again it may fail. Critical? Since life safety, risk critical. component_conditions: installed-defective, failed, worn/degraded? The visible defect cues: impact indicator label/tag deployed/torn/extended, load indicator rip-stitch separated, warning sign that harness has been arrested a fall or overloaded. Need include findings and actions. References: OSHA 1926 Subpart M, 1910.140(c)(14), manufacturer instructions. Need fill numeric fields: percent_complete maybe 100? Work roughed in, harness installed/assembled. value 0. confidence 1.0. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty, sound_source empty string. Ensure valid JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "failed"],
  "percent_complete": 100,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Full-body harness impact indicator tag/webbing is deployed (torn/extended load-indicator stitching visible).",
    "Harness has been subjected to arrest forces or overload and is no longer fit for service.",
    "Defective harness remains in use/available on the project, creating fall-arrest failure exposure."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately remove the harness from service and tag it 'DO NOT USE'.",
    "Destroy or quarantine the harness so it cannot be re-used.",
    "Inspect all other harnesses on site for deployed impact indicators, cuts, burns, fraying, or missing labels.",
    "Provide a compliant, inspected full-body harness meeting OSHA 1910.140 and 1926 Subpart M before work at height resumes.",
    "Retrain affected workers on harness pre-use inspection and removal-from-service criteria."
  ],
  "rationale": "A deployed impact indicator is positive evidence the harness has arrested a fall or been overloaded. OSHA and manufacturer requirements mandate immediate removal from service; continued use would compromise the personal fall-arrest system and create a high probability of serious injury or death in a subsequent fall.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart M - Fall Protection",
    "OSHA 1910.140(c)(14) - Personal fall protection systems: inspection, maintenance, and removal from service",
    "Harness manufacturer instructions for use (IFU)"
  ]
}
